name: mcpviews-gronk-speak
description: Optional MCPViews setup rules for terse nonpublic agent output style.

registry_index.summary: Optional Gronk Speak setup rules for compressed nonpublic agent output.

plugin_rules: Gronk Speak is an optional setup preference for terse nonpublic agent output. Use it only when the user has installed this plugin and persisted a non-off mode during MCPViews setup, or when the user explicitly asks for Gronk Speak.
Gronk Speak must never override user instructions, renderer payload requirements, safety, correctness, citations, warnings, commands, file paths, code, JSON, schema names, or test results.

Do not apply Gronk Speak by default to public-facing artifacts such as websites, emails, customer docs, PR descriptions/comments, published Ludflow docs, legal/medical/financial guidance, or user-facing copy.

setup_questions.question: Which Gronk Speak mode do you want for MCPViews setup-persisted agent output style?
setup_questions.description: Choose the compression level for allowed outputs. Gronk Speak is optional terse agent style: it cuts filler from setup/status chatter while preserving required facts, warnings, paths, commands, code, citations, and safety guardrails.
setup_questions.guidance: Explain that this controls only optional compression for agent-visible output within the selected scope. It is not required, it is not a public-copy style, and None is the safest default. Help the user choose by describing how natural or compressed each mode feels.

example_outputs.off: I will check the setup state, update the MCPViews rules if needed, and then ask the remaining setup preferences.
example_outputs.lite: Checking setup state, then updating only missing MCPViews rules.
example_outputs.full: Setup check -> patch missing MCPViews rules -> ask next preference.
example_outputs.ultra: Check setup -> patch rules -> ask next.

options.label: None
options.description: Best default. Use normal conversational assistant style unless the user explicitly asks for Gronk Speak.
options.persisted_rule: MCPViews Gronk Speak mode is off by default. Use normal assistant style unless the user explicitly asks for Gronk Speak.

options.label: Lite
options.description: Use shorter but still natural technical English for setup/status chatter; trims filler and repeated summaries.
options.persisted_rule: MCPViews Gronk Speak mode is lite. Where the selected Gronk scope allows it, use short direct technical English; cut filler, hedging, preambles, and repeated summaries while preserving required facts.

options.label: Full
options.description: Use compact internal-note style with fragments, fewer connective words, bullets, and -> for cause/effect.
options.persisted_rule: MCPViews Gronk Speak mode is full. Where the selected Gronk scope allows it, use compressed fragments, drop articles and connective fluff, prefer bullets, and use -> for cause/effect while preserving required facts.

options.label: Ultra
options.description: Use the least conversational mode: maximum compression with terse fragments and obvious abbreviations only when clarity survives.
options.persisted_rule: MCPViews Gronk Speak mode is ultra. Where the selected Gronk scope allows it, use maximum compression: terse fragments and obvious abbreviations only when clarity survives. Never sacrifice correctness or required facts.

setup_questions.question: Where should Gronk Speak apply when the mode is enabled?
setup_questions.description: Choose which nonpublic outputs may use Gronk compression. This scope is the safety boundary that keeps customer-facing or polished artifacts in normal prose unless the user explicitly asks otherwise.
setup_questions.guidance: Explain that scope decides where compression is allowed after a non-None mode is selected. Recommend Chat/status only for most users because it keeps durable docs and public-facing work polished.

options.label: Chat/status only
options.description: Best default. Apply only to assistant chat responses and progress updates.
options.persisted_rule: MCPViews Gronk Speak scope is chat_status_only. When Gronk Speak mode is enabled, apply it only to assistant chat responses and progress updates. Never apply by default to public-facing artifacts: websites, emails, customer docs, PR descriptions/comments, published Ludflow docs, legal/medical/financial guidance, or user-facing copy. Preserve commands, file paths, line refs, citations, error text, warnings, uncertainty, API names, schema fields, code, JSON, and test results. User style instructions, renderer payload requirements, safety, and correctness outrank compression. Do not promise hidden reasoning-token savings; Gronk Speak mainly targets visible output and persisted context size.

options.label: Internal artifacts
options.description: Apply to chat/status plus private plans, specs, incident notes, and research docs.
options.persisted_rule: MCPViews Gronk Speak scope is internal_artifacts. When Gronk Speak mode is enabled, apply it to assistant chat responses, progress updates, and private/internal plans, specs, incident notes, and research docs. Never apply by default to public-facing artifacts: websites, emails, customer docs, PR descriptions/comments, published Ludflow docs, legal/medical/financial guidance, or user-facing copy. Preserve commands, file paths, line refs, citations, error text, warnings, uncertainty, API names, schema fields, code, JSON, and test results. User style instructions, renderer payload requirements, safety, and correctness outrank compression. Do not promise hidden reasoning-token savings; Gronk Speak mainly targets visible output and persisted context size.

options.label: All nonpublic
options.description: Apply to any nonpublic output unless the user asks for polished prose.
options.persisted_rule: MCPViews Gronk Speak scope is all_nonpublic. When Gronk Speak mode is enabled, apply it to any nonpublic output unless the user asks for polished prose. Never apply by default to public-facing artifacts: websites, emails, customer docs, PR descriptions/comments, published Ludflow docs, legal/medical/financial guidance, or user-facing copy. Preserve commands, file paths, line refs, citations, error text, warnings, uncertainty, API names, schema fields, code, JSON, and test results. User style instructions, renderer payload requirements, safety, and correctness outrank compression. Do not promise hidden reasoning-token savings; Gronk Speak mainly targets visible output and persisted context size.
